classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
When He Speaks of God’s Help, He Means It Only to Help Us Do What Without It We Still Could Do.
title: When He Speaks of God’s Help, He Means It Only to Help Us Do What Without It We Still Could Do.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 29 [XXVIII.]—When He Speaks of God’s Help, He Means It Only to Help Us Do What Without It We Still Could Do.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Again, in the first book of his Defence of the Freedom of the Will, he says: “But while we have within us a free will so strong and so stedfast against sinning, which our Maker has implanted in human nature generally, still, by His unspeakable goodness, we are further defended by His own daily help.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: What need is there of such help, if free will is so strong and so stedfast against sinning?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But here, as before, he would have it understood that the purpose of the alleged assistance is, that that may be more easily accomplished by grace which he nevertheless supposes may be effected, less easily, no doubt, but yet actually, without grace.
